samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH" value="8">Width of each data entry.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH" value="16">Depth of the FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instance
        <instance id="dual_port_RAM" module_id="dual_port_RAM">
            <connection id="wclk" port_id="wclk"/>
            <connection id="wenc" port_id="wen"/>
            <connection id="waddr" port_id="waddr"/>
            <connection id="wdata" port_id="wdata"/>
            <connection id="rclk" port_id="rclk"/>
            <connection id="renc" port_id="ren"/>
            <connection id="raddr" port_id="raddr"/>
            <connection id="rdata" port_id="rdata"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Data write controller logic
        <assign id="waddr_bin" expression="waddr_buff + winc"/>
        <assign id="wptr" expression="{waddr_buff[3], waddr_buff[2], waddr_buff[1], waddr_buff[0]} ^ {waddr_buff[3], waddr_buff[2], waddr_buff[1], waddr_buff[0] >> 1}"/>
        <always id="write_pointer_update">
            @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn)
            begin
                if (!wrstn)
                    waddr_buff <= 0;
                else
                    waddr_buff <= waddr_bin;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Data read controller logic
        <assign id="raddr_bin" expression="raddr_buff + rinc"/>
        <assign id="rptr" expression="{raddr_buff[3], raddr_buff[2], raddr_buff[1], raddr_buff[0]} ^ {raddr_buff[3], raddr_buff[2], raddr_buff[1], raddr_buff[0] >> 1}"/>
        <always id="read_pointer_update">
            @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn)
            begin
                if (!rrstn)
                    raddr_buff <= 0;
                else
                    raddr_buff <= raddr_bin;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Read pointer synchronizer logic
        <assign id="rptr_syn" expression="{rptr[3], rptr[2], rptr[1], rptr[0]} ^ {rptr[3], rptr[2], rptr[1], rptr[0] >> 1}"/>
        <always id="read_pointer_sync">
            @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn)
            begin
                if (!wrstn)
                    rptr_buff <= 0;
                else
                    rptr_buff <= rptr_syn;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Write pointer synchronizer logic
        <assign id="wptr_syn" expression="{wptr[3], wptr[2], wptr[1], wptr[0]} ^ {wptr[3], wptr[2], wptr[1], wptr[0] >> 1}"/>
        <always id="write_pointer_sync">
            @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn)
            begin
                if (!rrstn)
                    wptr_buff <= 0;
                else
                    wptr_buff <= wptr_syn;
            end
        </always>
        
        // Full and empty signal generation logic
        <assign id="wfull" expression="(wptr == ~rptr_buff[3:0]) ? 1 : 0"/>
        <assign id="rempty" expression="(rptr == wptr_buff) ? 1 : 0"/>
        
        // Input/output connections
        <assign id="wen" expression="winc && !wfull"/>
        <assign id="ren" expression="rinc && !rempty"/>
        <assign id="waddr" expression="waddr_buff"/>
        <assign id="raddr" expression="raddr_buff"/>
        <assign id="wdata" expression="wdata"/>
        <assign id="rdata" expression="rdata"/>
    </logic_description>
</module>
